name: NFL Mock Draft Virtual Competition
description: Inaugural NFL mock draft virtual competition for students. Simulates a front office experience for strategic roster management through scouting, trades, and careful player selection. Teams are randomly assigned an NFL team and control their picks per real draft positioning. Two-round draft with 5-minute pick windows, judged by sport industry professionals.
speakers: Dr. Mark Orlando – Assistant Professor & Director of Sports Management, CLU
Artis Twyman – VP Communications, Los Angeles Rams
extra_details: Teams of 4-6 people. Round 1: 8:30 AM – 11:10 AM. Break 11:10–11:30 AM. Round 2: 11:30 AM – 2:10 PM. Judging: 2:20–3:00 PM. Winners announced with trophies and prizes. Teams must submit survey outlines justifying every pick including statistical evaluation, team dynamics impact, behavioral attributes, branding appeal, and short/long-term objectives. Trades allowed with panel approval (dead cap, cap space rules enforced).

name: Fields of Opportunity: Score Your Sports Career
location: Soiland Multipurpose Arena – Gilbert Sports & Fitness Center, CLU
description: Sports career fair providing direct access to sport organizations and programming. Assists students in establishing professional relationships, discussing potential job, internship, and professional development opportunities. Many opportunities are exclusive to the Sports Management program’s established partnerships.
cost: Free for CLU attendees; $9.99 outside guests
extra_details: Participating organizations: Athletes to Careers (Programming), Bank of America Performing Arts Center (Volunteers), Beverly Hills Sports Council (Internships), BZA Sports PR & Marketing (Entry-Level/Internships/Volunteers), CLU Athletic Dept (Internships), CLU Basketball (Internships), CLU Sport Management Graduate Program, CLU Sports Information (Internships), CLU Tennis (Internships), COSM Los Angeles (Internships), IMPACT Learning Institute (Programming), LA Angels Publications (Internships), LA Angels (Inside Sales), LA Kings (Sales Academy), LA Rams 50/50 (Ticket Sales), LA Rams (Gameday Ambassadors), Reach Capacity (Internships/Programming), Santa Barbara Foresters (Gameday Ops/Writing/Social/Marketing/Outreach Internships), Spark and Sway (Marketing Internships), Special Olympics SoCal (Internships/Volunteers), The Sporting Tribune (Content), UC Santa Barbara (Sport Mgmt Certification), USF (Sport Mgmt Graduate Program), Ventura Vikings (Internships), West Valley Family YMCA (Part-time/Volunteers), Young Athletes for Christ (Internships), Zuma Triathlon (Internships), 24 Hour Fitness Thousand Oaks (Part-time/Training).

name: New Venture Fair 2026
location: Gilbert Sports & Fitness Center, Soiland Arena, CLU
description: Student teams share innovative startup projects in a competitive environment. The community gets its first look at CLU’s exceptional student entrepreneurs and votes for favorites. Teams from CLU main campus, Executive MBA in Europe, and multiple high schools.
contact: Steven Dorfman Center for Innovation & Entrepreneurship – (805) 493-3668
extra_details: Participating schools: CLU main campus, EMBA in Europe Program, Bakersfield Christian High School, Newbury Park High School, Oaks Christian School, Thousand Oaks High School, Westlake High School, Start-Up Kids, One Spark Academy. Key deadlines: March 1 (student applications), April 10 (sign deliverable), April 24 (all other deliverables), May 6 (event day). Competition guidelines and business idea submission available on website.

name: SOM Alumni Happy Hour Reunion
location: Kingsmen Park, Thousand Oaks Campus, CLU
description: Reconnect, celebrate, and raise a glass with fellow School of Management alumni. Graduates from across California alongside program directors, faculty, retired faculty, and friends of the School. Discounted tickets include access to the Graduation Banquet and two-course catered dinner.
extra_details: Features: VIP Check-In with commemorative Class Milestone pin and drink ticket. Dean’s Toast: fast-paced, high-energy update on initiatives. Class Flashback group alumni photos. Open networking before banquet at 5:30 PM. Children under 12 attend free (email organizer beforehand).

name: 10th Annual Graduation Banquet – Class of 2026
location: Kingsmen Park, Thousand Oaks Campus, CLU
description: Celebrate the achievements of the Class of 2026 at this milestone 10th Annual Graduate Banquet. Graduating students, families, and friends are warmly invited to gather for an evening of recognition, gratitude, and celebration.
cost: Free for graduating SOM students; guest early-bird pricing available (closed March 22, 2026)
extra_details: Features: Two-Course Dinner catered by Pierre’s Catering. Live Music & Dancing. Photo Booths. Silent Auction, Games & More. Children under 12 attend free (email organizer beforehand). Catered by Pierre’s Catering. Past sponsors include Disney, Capital, Sondoro, Arroyo Capital, Nissan.

name: Not All Those Who Wander Are Lost: James Werbe
description: James Werbe—filmmaker turned founder and CEO of Wayside Energy—shares his unconventional journey into entrepreneurship and how his background in the arts and philosophy shaped how he builds companies, leads teams, and tackles complex problems.
speakers: James Werbe – Founder & CEO, Wayside Energy
contact: Steven Dorfman Center for Innovation & Entrepreneurship
extra_details: Monthly Speaker Series hosted by the Dorfman Center. Opportunity for entrepreneurs and entrepreneurial-minded people to network and learn from experienced entrepreneurs. Format: keynote speaker followed by audience Q&A, networking before and after. Past speakers include Jeanne Sikoff (Zander’s Game House), Ian Hathaway (Far Out Ventures), Robert Pieroni (gener8tor), Petra Griffith (Wedbush Ventures). Next ESS event TBA.

name: 10th Annual ETMF: Talent Mobility & Workforce Agility
location: Lundring Events Center, CLU, 60 W. Olsen Rd, Thousand Oaks
description: Organizations must develop workforce agility and talent mobility strategies to remain competitive. Explores how businesses build a resilient workforce capable of adapting to rapid change amid economic uncertainties, geopolitical shifts, technological and environmental disruptions.
speakers: Ixora Aristigueta-Becerra – VP People & Workplace Experience, The Sobrato Organization
Dr. Vlad Vaiman – Professor, School of Management, CLU
Dr. Gabriella Sherman – Chief Medical Officer, Los Robles Health System
Bruce Stenslie – President & CEO, Economic Development Collaborative
Kristin Decas – CEO/Port Director, Port of Hueneme
Primo Custodio (moderator) – VP HR (ret.), NBC Universal
extra_details: Schedule: 8 AM Reception/Breakfast, 9 AM Dean’s Welcome, 9:15 Academic Presentation, 9:35 Practitioner Keynote, 10:15 Q&A, 10:30 Break, 10:45 Panel Discussion, 11:30 Panel Q&A, 11:55 Closing, 12 PM Lunch/Networking, 1 PM End. Sponsors: PennyMac, Sage, POH, VCEDA, City of TO, EDC Accelerate CA. Advisory Council: Dr. Vlad Vaiman, Susan Van Vleet, Nick Shauer, Primo Custodio, Kim Congdon. Next ETMF likely Sept/Oct 2026 – watch for announcement.

name: Life in Tune with Freddie Ravel
description: Grammy-nominated artist Freddie Ravel presents Life in Tune, a one-of-a-kind program designed to challenge and inspire organizations to discover their untapped potential through the language of music. Fast-paced, entertaining, full of surprises.
speakers: Freddie Ravel – Grammy-Nominated Artist, Keynote Presenter, LIFE IN TUNE Founder
extra_details: Forward Together brings interesting topics and people to the global community of students, alumni, and leaders. Moderated by Dean Gerhard Apfelthaler. Past speakers include John Maier (CEO Bose Professional), Chris Lytton (President, Jim Henson Company), Joe Hadley (Spotify), Anna Wikland (Google Sweden), and dozens more covering topics from cryptocurrency to sustainability to crisis management. Next webinar TBA.

name: 2026 Ventura County Economic Forecast
description: Broken Inheritance: How Today’s Decisions Handicap the Next Generation. Matthew Fienup presents the 2026 Ventura County Economic Forecast with in-depth analysis of the county’s economic performance and the role of local policies in shaping growth, plus detailed discussion of Ventura County agriculture.
speakers: Matthew Fienup, PhD – Executive Director, CERF
Veronique de Rugy, PhD – Chair of Political Economy, George Mason University; nationally syndicated columnist
cost: $75 early admission / $105 at the door; Free with Cal Lutheran ID
extra_details: CERF provides world-class economic research and forecasting. Matthew Fienup is frequently quoted in Wall Street Journal, Barron’s, MarketWatch, NBC News, CNBC. Speaking engagements bookable through Tim Gallagher, Gallagher PR – (805) 276-2110 – timgal2@gmail.com. Past forecasts: 2025, 2024, 2023 available on website.

name: Early Childhood Education & Benefits for CA Economy
description: Explores the long-term impact of early childhood development on a society. Dr. Jamshid Damooei, who has studied ECD for decades, is joined by Fabiola Lara, founder of EquaNurture, for this policy-focused talk.
speakers: Dr. Jamshid Damooei – Professor of Economics, CLU
contact: Dr. Sabith Khan, MPPA Program Director
extra_details: MPPA Policy Talks brings fresh, original perspectives on issues of policy, administration and governance. Past topics include U.S. Foreign Policy in the Middle East (Dr. Khodr Zaarour), Social and Solidarity Economy (Dr. Eric Griego), California Water Policies, and more. Next MPPA talk TBA.

name: DEIJB in the Workplace Series
description: Panel discussions on diversity, equity, inclusion, justice, and belonging in the workplace. Professionals and students share stories about entering the workforce, unexpected challenges, actions taken, and advice for attendees.
extra_details: Last event: Overcoming Barriers & Navigating Success (Oct 10, 2023) with Rafael Cordero, Brittney Elhard, Mark Garcia, Karen D. King. Moderated by John Garcia. Earlier: People with Disabilities in the Workforce (April 18, 2023) with Cindy Liu, Jacob Lesner-Buxton, Matthew Lowe. Future topics planned: Gender Gap Issues, Age Discrimination, Inequalities, Cultural Differences, Differences in Abilities.

name: TechTalk Speaker Series
description: Quarterly speaker series providing students access to leading voices in the IT industry. Interactive sessions encourage exchange between students and industry professionals to learn from each other, explore career paths, and highlight opportunities for innovative applications of technology.
contact: Dr. Earle Charles / Dr. Paul Witman, MSIT Program
extra_details: Last event: Preparing For The AI Integrated Workplace (Nov 13, 2024) with Aaron Anderson, President of Brandes Associates. Previous topics: AI Strategies From Corporate Technology Leaders (Nov 2023, John Motz CTO NetDocuments + Casey Stoops Patagonia IT), Technology in Media & Entertainment (May 2022, Nina Skorus-Neely), Philosophy/Science/Technology (Feb 2022, Dr. Sharena Rice), Data Science (Nov 2021, Dr. Jeremy Greene), Mental Health/Tech/Leadership (Aug 2021, CeCe Morken CEO Headspace). Next TechTalk TBA.

name: Pathways & Possibilities: How to Succeed IRL
description: For over 25 years, this event has guided CLU students on career journeys. Led by SOM students, it connects attendees with alumni and professionals through inspiring keynotes, hands-on workshops, and networking. Helps align skills, passions, and values to career goals.
extra_details: Latest keynote: Timmy ‘The Dreamer’ – youth keynote speaker, founder of Dreamer State University, former business partner with Charlie Rocket (9M followers, collaborated with Oprah, Steph Curry, Mr Beast, Jake Paul, MGK). Format: Keynote + Workshop (5:30 PM), Roundtable Discussions (6:45 PM), Open Networking (7:30 PM). Recent roundtable professionals: Jake Aguilera (Financial Advisor), Jon Baker (Global CIO, former Disney VP), Andrea Brimmer (Founder Hortitech), Billy Carlson (UX iHerb), Deanna Grady (LinkedIn Alumni Leader), Andrew Kiefer (CBIZ), Sylvia Kokiousis (Answer Financial), Tyler Lucas (AltSpot Capital), Brendon Norton (VP Tekmetric), Jeannette Pugh (Cedars-Sinai), J. Shakir Ramsey (Google), Briana Shneyder (Netflix), Timothy Thompson (LA Rams), Charline Zeroual (Fashion Consultant). Next event TBA.
